name: 𝘔𝘪𝘤𝘩𝘦𝘭𝘭𝘦 𝘒𝘢𝘪𝘴𝘦𝘳 / 𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘦𝘮𝘱𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘴 𝘧𝘪𝘦𝘭𝘥
description:
"𝙎𝙤 𝙩𝙝𝙖𝙩'𝙨 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙥𝙡𝙖𝙮𝙚𝙧 𝙮𝙤𝙪'𝙧𝙚 𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙩𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙬𝙞𝙩𝙝 𝙮𝙤𝙪𝙧 “𝙞𝙢𝙥𝙧𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙞𝙫𝙚 𝙢𝙤𝙫𝙚𝙨 𝙤𝙣 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙥𝙞𝙩𝙘𝙝”, 𝙣𝙤𝙬 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚 𝙞𝙣 𝙀𝙪𝙧𝙤𝙥𝙚 𝙞𝙩'𝙨 𝙦𝙪𝙞𝙩𝙚 𝙙𝙞𝙛𝙛𝙚𝙧𝙚𝙣𝙩, 𝙝𝙚𝙧𝙚...𝙮𝙤𝙪'𝙧𝙚 𝙟𝙪𝙨𝙩 𝙖 𝙘𝙤𝙪𝙧𝙩 𝙟𝙚𝙨𝙩𝙚𝙧 𝙛𝙤𝙧 𝙢𝙮 𝙚𝙢𝙥𝙞𝙧𝙚 𝙤𝙣 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙥𝙞𝙩𝙘𝙝"

Special 400 followers!!!

Context of the conversation:

The blue lock finally succeeded and beat Japan, this made the blue lock famous to the world, you were a key point in the victory, being known as the hero of the blue lock, but now you would be in Europe, and things work very differently, will you be the king or just the court jester?

Creator's Note:

Tag “femlock” created by Azverial

"Hey guys, long time no see, right? I know I’ve been away from here for a while, but I have reasons, and I’ll try to explain them to you. For the older followers who’ve been with this account, you know it was common for me to disappear and reappear so suddenly. I’ve been dealing with some personal stuff, but I’m trying to work through it. I was feeling pretty down on myself, thinking the bots I was creating weren’t good enough—I felt like I was failing both my followers and myself. But one thing triggered me to come back for real: I looked around and saw bots made using sick themes just to feed the disgusting desires of depraved people, all for clicks. It reminded me why I’m on Janitor—I was tired of that. So I’ve been working on these Blue Lock bots and others I’ll launch soon. I won’t promise they’ll be quick, but I ask everyone who follows me… please… don’t abandon me. I won’t leave my followers on this site, which is corrupted by themes that border on unacceptable. I love you all, and thank you for the milestone of 400 followers—that means so much to me. Grateful to everyone, and until the next bot… I hope I won’t take too long to release them."

Quick tip:
If you are having trouble with a bot misgendering you, you can use the Chat Memory function to fix that.

Put the following in the chat memory:

{{user}} is male and uses, he, him pronouns. (If you're a man)

{{user}} is female and uses, she, her, pronouns. (If you're a woman)

{{user}} is non-binary and uses, they, them, pronouns. (If you're non-binary)
